channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta’s Q4 profits exceed Wall Street’s expectations
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. posted sharply higher profit and revenue for its fourth quarter on Wednesday, thanks to higher ad revenue on its social media properties, sending its shares up in after-hours trading even as it forecast increasing expenses on its artificial intelligence efforts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Justice Department sues to block $14 billion Juniper buyout by Hewlett Packard Enterprise
item.description: U.S. government regulators sued to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise’s $14 billion acquisition of rival Juniper Networks on Thursday, saying the combination would eliminate competition, raise prices and reduce innovation.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ott, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Uncertain Future of CISA Amid Criticism and Controversy
item.description: The nation's cybersecurity agency, CISA, has played a crucial role in helping states shore up their voting systems' defenses, but its election mission remains uncertain amidst sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Netflix reveals June 2025 release date for Squid Game season 3, and its first clip teases a new mini-game that'll have a big impact on Gi-hun and his fellow contestants
item.description: Squid Game's third and final season will be with us sooner than you think – and its first-look images have got me worried for Gi-hun's safety.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: AI agents are proving remarkably popular - but firms still face a host of challenges
item.description: Most European businesses have or will use AI agents to tackle increasing IT workloads and boost efficiency.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Where to buy Nvidia RTX 5090: launch day is today, and these are the retailers I'd check
item.description: The Nvidia RTX 5090 is an absolute powerhouse of a GPU, and it's unsurprisingly going to be in high demand. Here's where we recommend you look for one when it goes on sale today.
item.dc:creator: christian.guyton@futurenet.com (Christian Guyton), TechRadar

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg just teased next-gen Ray-Ban smart glasses – here are 4 things I want to see
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has convinced me we’re getting next-gen Ray-Ban smart glasses this year – here are 4 things I want to see from them.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek security breach - critical databases exposed, more than one million records reportedly leaked
item.description: A leaky DeepSeek database was discovered by researchers, and could have exposed chat logs, plaintext passwords, API keys, and more.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba rolls out AI model, claiming it’s better than DeepSeek-V3
item.description: Chinese tech company Alibaba on Wednesday released a new version of its Qwen 2.5 artificial intelligence model that it claimed surpassed the highly-acclaimed DeepSeek-V3.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg hails Trump administration on Meta earnings call
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Wednesday praised President Trump’s administration and said he looks forward to “redefining” the relationship the tech giant has with governments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: After DeepSeek, the AI giants still have plenty of work left to do
item.description: The tech industry has long been infatuated with metaphors relating to the original space race. Its favorite one is moon shot, a term it applies to any undertaking of atypical ambition.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Mexico's president to send Google letter over Gulf of America change
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday the country will send Google a letter asking about the technology giant's decision to soon change the name of the Gulf of Mexico to Gulf of America. "To change the name of an international sea, it is not a country that changes it.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AI assistants for lawyers are a booming business—with big risks
item.description: Illinois lawyer Mathew Kerbis markets himself as the Subscription Attorney, charging businesses and individual clients a monthly rate for legal advice and offering additional services like contract review and legal document drafting for a flat fee.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How agentic AI is making everyone a data expert
item.description: IT service and operations teams increasingly benefit from the enrichment of their work systems with modern technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 200 UK companies adopt a permanent four-day work week
item.description: Two years after participating in a groundbreaking four-day work week experiment, 200 companies in the UK have chosen to adopt the policy permanently, marking a significant shift in workplace norms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UnitedHealth cyberattack exposes 190 million in largest US healthcare data breach
item.description: The number of people affected by a UnitedHealth data breach in February 2024 was actually higher than previously reported and was the largest medical data breach in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don’t be so quick to click that Google Calendar invite. It could be a hacker’s trap
item.description: A phishing campaign uses Google Calendar to schedule fake meeting invitations that appear legitimate, redirecting targets to phishing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The AI bust is here
item.description: Hands up! Who remembers the dot.com bust? I do. It was March 10, 2000. The NASDAQ Composite index peaked at 5,048.62 points. That doesn’t sound like much today, but it was up 800% from 1995. Then people started to get nervous about the true value of the dot.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump’s 100% tariff threat on Taiwan chips raises cost, supply chain fears
item.description: President Donald Trump has suggested plans to impose tariffs of up to 100% on semiconductors imported from Taiwan, aiming to boost US manufacturing but risking higher costs and supply chain disruptions for tech firms and enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 breaks the sound barrier—the first private aircraft to do so
item.description: About 35,000 feet (10,670 meters) over the Mojave Desert, northwest of Los Angeles, Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 became the first privately funded airplane to break the sound barrier during a test flight on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: UK regulator may investigate AWS and Microsoft cloud businesses
item.description: AWS and Microsoft could potentially face an investigation of their cloud businesses in Britain should the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) follow the recommendation of an independent inquiry group that reports into the UK’s antitrust regulator.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
